workflow.name: Send timezone-aware drip emails with Gmail and Google Sheets using daily limits
workflow.description:
## How it works

- Three scheduled triggers fire on weekdays at region-appropriate working hours: EU/UK at 10:00 UTC, North America at 18:00 UTC, and Australia at 01:00 UTC
- All three feed one shared pipeline, so there is no duplicated logic across regions
- A code node detects the active region from the current UTC hour and picks a daily send limit (45 / 90 / 15 by default)
- Reads your contact list from Google Sheets, filters by country-to-region mapping, skips rows already marked sent, and caps at the daily limit
- Sends a personalised email via Gmail using expressions like `{{ $json['First Name'] }}` and `{{ $json['Company'] }}`
- Writes success or error status back to the same row (`email_sent`, `sent_date`, `status`, `group_sent`, `error_msg`)
- No `Wait` nodes, no long-running executions - timing is driven entirely by Cron schedules

## Set up steps

- Setup takes about 10-15 minutes
- Connect a **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential with read + write access to your contacts sheet
- Connect a **Gmail OAuth2** credential for the sending account
- Create a Google Sheet with these columns: `First Name`, `Company`, `Email`, `Country`, `email_sent`, `sent_date`, `status`, `group_sent`, `error_msg`
- In all three Google Sheets nodes, select your spreadsheet and the contacts tab
- Open the **Build Email** node and replace the placeholder subject and HTML body with your own copy
- In the **Send Gmail** node, set `senderName` to your display name
- Optional: adjust daily send caps inside **Determine Group** and edit the country-to-region lookup inside **Filter and Limit**
- Detailed per-step notes live inside the workflow as sticky notes

workflow.name: Timezone-aware email drip campaign with daily send limits (no wait nodes) - Gmail + Google Sheets

Hi {{ $json['First Name'] }},

[Explain the problem you help solve, in one short paragraph.]

[Your product or service name] helps [target persona] to [outcome], without [common pain point].

Happy to share more if relevant - feel free to reply with "yes" and I will send details.

Best regards,

[Your Name] - [Your Company]
[Your title]
https://example.com

parameters.content:
## Timezone-aware email drip campaign (no wait nodes)

Send outreach emails at the right working hour for each audience region, with a daily cap per region, status tracking in Google Sheets, and per-row error handling. No `Wait` nodes required - timing is driven entirely by three region-specific Cron schedules.

### What it does
- Three schedule triggers fire at the local working-hours window of each region (EU/UK, NA, AU)
- A shared code node inspects the current UTC hour and picks the matching region + daily send limit
- Reads your contact sheet, filters by country -> region mapping, skips rows already marked sent, and caps at the daily limit
- Sends a personalised Gmail message
- Writes `email_sent`, `sent_date`, `status`, `group_sent`, and `error_msg` back to the same row

### Credentials required
- **Google Sheets OAuth2** - reads contacts and writes status back
- **Gmail OAuth2** - sends the outreach email

### Setup (10-15 min)
1. Create a Google Sheet with the columns listed in the "Sheet schema" sticky note
2. In all three **Google Sheets** nodes, pick your sheet and the contacts tab
3. Edit the **Build Email** node: replace the placeholder subject line and HTML body with your own copy
4. In the **Send Gmail** node, set `senderName` to your name
5. Optional: adjust the daily caps inside **Determine Group** (currently 45 / 90 / 15)
6. Optional: edit the country -> region mapping inside **Filter and Limit** to match your contact list
7. Activate the workflow

## Step 1 - Three timezone-specific triggers

Three independent `Schedule Trigger` nodes fire on weekdays (`Mon-Fri`):

- **EU_UK** at 10:00 UTC  (morning in Europe)
- **NA** at 18:00 UTC     (late morning on US East coast)
- **AU** at 01:00 UTC     (noon in eastern Australia)

All three feed into the same `Determine Group` node, so there is exactly ONE shared pipeline instead of three copies.

Edit the cron expressions to match your preferred send windows.

## Step 2 - Determine region + daily cap

Reads the current UTC hour and returns:
- `group`: one of `EU_UK`, `NA`, `AU`
- `daily_limit`: max emails for that region today (default 45 / 90 / 15)

Ranges are non-overlapping so each trigger maps to exactly one region. Adjust the numbers here to change your send volume per region.

## Step 3 - Read contacts from Google Sheets

Reads every row from your contacts tab. You must:
1. Select your own spreadsheet in this node after import
2. Pick the tab containing the contact list

See the "Sheet schema" sticky note for the exact columns expected.

## Step 4 - Filter by region, skip already-sent, cap at daily limit

For the active `group`:
- Matches each row's `Country` against the country -> region lookup in the code
- Drops rows whose `email_sent` column is not empty
- Drops rows without a valid `@` in the `Email` column
- Slices the result down to the region's `daily_limit`

Add or remove countries in the `countryGroups` object inside this node to match your audience.

## Step 5 - Build personalised email

A `Set` node assembles the outgoing email into these fields:
- `to` - the recipient address
- `subject` - subject line (supports expressions like `{{ $json['Company'] }}`)
- `body` - HTML body (supports `{{ $json['First Name'] }}` and any other column)
- `group_sent` - recorded later in the sheet for audit

Replace the placeholder subject and HTML body with your own copy.

## Step 6 - Send via Gmail

Sends through your connected Gmail account. Set `senderName` in the node options to your display name.

`onError: continueRegularOutput` means a failed send does NOT stop the batch - the error object flows downstream so the failed row can be logged separately.

## Step 7 - Success / error branching

The `IF` node checks whether the Gmail response contains an `error` object:
- **true (error exists)** -> `Update Row Error` writes `status = failed` + the error message
- **false (no error)** -> `Update Row Success` writes `status = sent`

Both branches match on the `Email` column so the right row is updated.

## Sheet schema

Create a tab (e.g. `Contacts`) with these columns in the first row:

- `First Name`
- `Company`  *(used in the subject placeholder; optional if you rewrite the subject)*
- `Email`
- `Country`  *(must match a key in the country -> region map)*
- `email_sent`   *(leave blank; filled by the workflow)*
- `sent_date`    *(leave blank)*
- `status`       *(leave blank)*
- `group_sent`   *(leave blank)*
- `error_msg`    *(leave blank)*

### Supported countries out of the box
EU_UK: Austria, Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Norway, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom
NA: Canada, United States
AU: Australia

Add more inside the `Filter and Limit` node.
